mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate signals a peak economic cycle and potential for wage inflation, leading to market expectations of tighter monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% as of 2006-12-01 is near historical lows for the preceding decade.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the federal funds rate serves as a key component of the discount rate for future corporate earnings. A higher rate directly increases the discount factor, reducing the present value of equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.24% as of 2006-12-01 is considered restrictive by historical standards.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent slowdown in housing construction and retail sales indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand, which negatively impacts forward earnings expectations for a large portion of the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average change in HOUST is negative AND 3-month average change in RSAFS is negative.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market has been showing significant signs of weakness since mid-2006.
failure_condition: When both series show negative 3-month momentum, the S&P 500 has positive 60-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects strengthening corporate activity and output. This leads to higher expected earnings and positive equity returns as the market prices in economic expansion.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment, an upside surprise in inflation heightens market fears of further monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and causing a rapid, negative repricing of equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve has been in a tightening cycle to combat inflation concerns.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, S&P 500 5-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time following a new CPI release.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust economy, supporting aggregate demand and corporate revenues, which translates to positive equity market performance over the following month.
failure_condition: Periods of high 3-month average payroll growth (top quartile) are not followed by statistically significant positive 20-day S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP is a strong signal of a potential recession, causing investors to de-risk portfolios and sell equities in anticipation of a broad-based decline in corporate earnings.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following the release of a negative real GDP growth print, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a tight labor market and above-target inflation forces the central bank to maintain restrictive monetary policy, suppressing equity valuations through a higher discount rate and slower expected growth.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve operates under a dual mandate of price stability and maximum employment.
failure_condition: A strategy of selling the S&P 500 when the trigger conditions are met fails to generate a negative information ratio over a 5-year walk-forward period.
